front: Play: the title character's exclamation of "merde!" (with an extra R) caused an uproar at the premiere; later staged with marionettes
Play: the cowardly title king frequently swears "by my green candle"
Play: a send-up of Macbeth in which the title king usurps the throne of Poland and goes to war with Russia; followed by sequels titled "Cuckolded" and "in Chains"
Play: W.B. Yeats lamented "after us, the Savage God" after seeing this play's premiere
Play: Paul Sérusier and Pierre Bonnard helped design the set for the premiere of this absurdist play
Play: ends with the title character fleeing to France; Bougrelas and Bordure avenge the death of King Wenceslas of Poland
Play: a woodcut by the author depicts the title character in a dunce cap with a spiral affixed to his bloated stomach
Playwright: invented 'pataphysics, the "science of imaginary solutions" that "explains the universe supplementary to this one"
work: Exploits and Opinions of Dr. Faustroll / 'Pataphysics

front: Novel: a picaresque novel featuring the laughing baboon Bosse-de-Nage as companion to a doctor of 'pataphysics
back: Exploits and Opinions of Dr. Faustroll (Alfred Jarry)
work: Exploits and Opinions of Dr. Faustroll / 'Pataphysics

front: Playwright: the Beraldi Album includes this author's sketch of two men clapping before a burning house
work: Visual Art and Influence on Artists

front: Playwright: Dora Maar captured a photograph of an armadillo fetus inspired by this author
work: Visual Art and Influence on Artists

front: Playwright: Rachilde, a French Decadent author, was a close friend and established the myth about the riotous opening night of this author's most famous play
Playwright: also wrote The Passion Considered as an Uphill Bicycle Race
